answers.text: Displays nothing and continues execution without errors.
Displays NULL because no rows are affected.
Displays 0 and the block ends normally.

question: Global variables declared in a package specification:

answers.text: Are visible only in the package body.
Cannot be initialized in the declaration.
Persist for the duration of the current session and can store state between calls to any subprogram in the package.
Are created at log-on and destroyed at commit.

question: Using the FORALL clause instead of a classic FOR loop is recommended for:

answers.text: Explicit support for VARRAY type collections.
Reducing the number of context switches between the PL/SQL engine and the SQL engine.
Allowing the use of GOTO inside DML statements.

question: Using the FORALL clause instead of a classic FOR loop is recommended for:

answers.text: Reducing the number of context switches between the PL/SQL engine and the SQL engine.
Explicit support for VARRAY type collections.
Allowing the use of GOTO inside DML statements.

question: A trigger that does not contain the FOR EACH ROW clause executes:

answers.text: Once for the DML statement, regardless of the number of affected rows.
Only if the statement affects at least one row.
Twice (before and after) for each row.

question: Which PL/SQL loop automatically handles OPEN, FETCH, and CLOSE operations for an explicit cursor?

answers.text: FOR i IN 1..SQL%ROWCOUNT LOOP ... END LOOP
FOR r IN c LOOP ... END LOOP

question: Select the true statement about a VARRAY:

answers.text: It is always a sparse collection.
It can contain a variable number of elements, but does not exceed a maximum size fixed at definition.
DELETE[n] can remove a single element.
The index can have negative values.
It cannot be passed as a parameter to a subprogram.

question: Which combination is valid regarding the existence of specification and/or body?

answers.text: Package with body but without specification – only for private local procedures.
If the specification is missing, the body automatically becomes public.
If the specification is missing, the body will not compile.
Package with only specification (no body) – possible when no implementation is needed; package with only body – allowed, but objects remain private.

question: Inside a trigger, the test IF INSERTING THEN ... is available:

answers.text: In any trigger (row or statement) to distinguish the DML event that triggered it.
